title: Race-Regime Mixture of Experts
summary: Dry races, wet races, and special rule contexts do not share the same stopping logic.
rationale: Dry races, wet races, and special rule contexts do not share the same stopping logic. Official rules and tyre guidance show different mandatory-compound behavior in dry conditions and a Monaco-specific 2025 two-stop exception.
implementation_hint: Train separate experts for at least: dry slick races, wet/intermediate races, and dry-rule-pressure cases; optionally isolate Monaco 2025 if present. Gate by compound family, race identity/year, and rule-state features such as dry-compounds-used and laps-to-finish.
expected_effect: Should reduce averaging across incompatible strategy regimes and improve tail cases where a single global model miscalibrates stop urgency.
risk: Sample sizes for rare regimes may be small; only keep regimes that beat the global model on race-held-out CV.
